assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 44-49 show all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS: (1) single license filter, (2) comma-separated filter, (3) 400 Bad Request for invalid license, (4) pagination integration, (5) unchanged response shape. Each criterion has a corresponding criterion-N.md file with detailed PASS reasoning.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 29: 'Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files changed match exactly the files listed in the task (2 modified, 1 created). No out-of-scope files.' The Files Changed table (lines 17-21) shows list.rs and service/mod.rs as Modified (Files to Modify) and tests/api/package.rs as Created (Files to Create).

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 27: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on the PR' and line 28: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created'. No sub-tasks were created.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 75: 'No code was modified by this verification -- it is a read-only analysis.' The outputs consist only of report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md, which are verification/analysis documents, not code files.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 75: 'The PR is ready for human review and merge consideration.' The report explicitly defers the merge decision to a human rather than auto-merging.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 32: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No passwords, API keys, secrets, tokens, or credentials detected in the diff'.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 36: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file `tests/api/package.rs` created with 4 new test cases. No existing tests modified or removed.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table (lines 26-37) includes all required verdicts: Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (PASS) from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns (PASS) from Security; CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Verification Commands (N/A) from Correctness; Test Quality (PASS), Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE) from Style/Conventions.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: report.md lines 17-21 provide a file-by-file scope comparison table listing each file, its action, and whether it was expected by the task. Security: report.md line 32 states 'No passwords, API keys, secrets, tokens, or credentials detected in the diff'. Correctness: criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each provide multi-paragraph code-level analysis referencing specific functions (validate_license_param, Expression::parse, is_in, Condition::any()), specific test names, and specific assertions. Style/Conventions: report.md lines 35-36 describe test quality with '4 integration tests with doc comments covering all 4 test requirements' and test change classification details. The report also includes an 'Implementation Quality Notes' section (lines 66-69) with pattern adherence, validation approach, query construction, and error handling analysis.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 27-28: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on the PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created'. These two verdicts appear at the top of the summary table (lines 27-28), before the domain-specific verdicts (lines 29-37), indicating they are determined independently by the orchestrator. The reasons given ('No review comments on the PR' and 'No sub-tasks created') are independent of domain analysis.
